items.description: Arabic numeral '57' written in the upper right corner in a smaller, lighter hand distinct from the main text.
items.scholarly_note: Later foliation or pagination mark, almost certainly post-medieval (16th–19th c.); such numbers were routinely added when the codex was bound, rebound, or catalogued.

items.description: Large calligraphic capital 'S' (approximately two lines high) with prominent leftward and downward flourishes at the start of the lower textual block.
items.scholarly_note: Scribal decorated initial marking a new section or stanza group within the Eddic poem; exaggerated flourishes and swelling strokes are characteristic of the main Codex Regius hand in high-grade Gothic book script.

items.description: Extended pen flourish from the large 'S' projecting leftward beyond the main text column into the margin.
items.scholarly_note: Integral part of the calligraphic initial; such marginal intrusions are common in this manuscript's decorative practice and do not represent later additions.

items.description: Small isolated curved ink stroke with a looped or blot-like terminal, located in the outer margin roughly at mid-page height adjacent to the lower lines of the upper text block.
items.scholarly_note: Pen trial or accidental ink deposit from quill testing or page handling; does not align with text and shows no linguistic content.

items.description: Small dark spot of concentrated ink or pigment immediately to the right of the text block in the middle of the page.
items.scholarly_note: Iron-gall ink splatter or offset from the writing process or contact with an adjacent leaf; typical and non-significant for textual content.

items.description: Minor localized discoloration and slightly denser ink area within the text lines near the middle of the upper block.
items.scholarly_note: Surface stain or minor ink corrosion/bleed; coincides with areas of lower OCR in automated processing and may represent either a scribal hesitation blot or simple pigment pooling.

items.description: Faint reversed or ghost lettering and smudged ink traces visible along the far right edge and in some interlinear spaces.
items.scholarly_note: Show-through from the verso or offset ink from the facing page; common in the thinner vellum leaves of this composite codex and does not constitute deliberate marginalia.

scratches: Fine linear abrasions and hair-side preparation marks visible in the lower right margin and in the blank space below the final line of text.
Short surface scuff or light scratch running diagonally in the right margin near the transition between the two main text blocks.
Minor scuffing and fiber disturbance along the bottom edge of the page.
damage_notes: Irregular trimmed or worn edges, especially right and bottom; lower right corner is rounded with slight material loss.
Natural vellum discoloration, grain, and faint cockling visible, more pronounced in the raw capture than in cleaned versions.
Scattered small dark spots and localized iron-gall corrosion; no major wormholes, large tears, or repairs observed on this page.
summary: Page 60 of Codex Regius (GKS 2365 4to) shows a generally clean writing surface with dense Gothic book hand containing Eddic heroic verse. The principal non-text features are a later-added foliation '57' in the upper right, a prominent flourished scribal initial 'S' marking a textual division, a few small pen trials and ink stains in the right margin, faint offset/show-through along the outer edge, and minor surface abrasions plus edge wear consistent with well-prepared but handled calf vellum of c. 1270. No faces, animals, elaborate doodles, interlinear glosses, or substantial scribal corrections are visible; the page is relatively free of post-production additions compared with more heavily annotated leaves in the codex.
